Confidence reduced because exact CF-runtime body-transfer semantics for `new Request(url, request)` + later `.clone()` need a runtime check, and the path is gated on body-token + Turnstile-enabled.

findings.subsystem: edge-control-plane (deploy/cloudflare/morpheus-edge-gateway + morpheus-control-plane)
findings.title: Control-plane ingress has no request body size cap before JSON.parse
findings.evidence: deploy/cloudflare/morpheus-control-plane/worker.mjs:143-149 does `const rawBody = await request.text(); payload = rawBody ? JSON.parse(rawBody) : {};` with no Content-Length / byte-length guard. The whole payload is then persisted verbatim into Supabase (`payload` column, worker.mjs:178) and re-enqueued into the Cloudflare queue (recovery.js:77-89 / worker.mjs:214-225). An oversized or deeply-nested body is read fully into memory, parsed, stored, and queued. Rate limiting is per-route/IP and keyed on a spoofable header fallback chain (getClientIp falls back to x-real-ip/x-forwarded-for in packages/shared/src/utils.js:34-41), so a single authenticated/anonymous-allowed client can push large bodies.
findings.fix: Before request.text(), reject when Content-Length exceeds a cap (e.g. 64-256KB, env-tunable) with json(413,{error:'payload_too_large'}); also bound the post-read rawBody.length. Cheap defense against memory/queue/Supabase-row bloat. Note: the cf-connecting-ip primary in getClientIp is CF-trustworthy, so the spoof concern is only the fallback path — low severity.
findings.risk: Choose the cap above the largest legitimate oracle/compute payload to avoid rejecting valid requests; make it env-configurable.

findings.subsystem: edge-control-plane (deploy/cloudflare/morpheus-edge-gateway + morpheus-control-plane)
findings.title: Emergency-vercel-runtime shim and the live box are not wired to a single source of truth for runtime mode; status divergence is silent
findings.evidence: apps/web/app/api/emergency-runtime/[...path]/route.ts hard-codes degraded health (`degraded:true, reason:'phala_runtime_control_plane_disabled'`, lines 55-56) and returns 503 for every non-health/info path (line 92). The edge gateway's runtime-status snapshot (worker.mjs:375-398) is built from probing the ORIGIN /health and /info, independently of this Vercel shim. There is no shared flag indicating which runtime is actually live, so when the box is up the gateway reports healthy while the Vercel emergency route still advertises degraded (and vice versa) — operators/consoles can get contradictory status from the two public surfaces with no reconciliation or alert. The shim's `oracle.target_chains`/`pricefeed_chain` are also hard-coded duplicates of the runtime catalog (worker.mjs:4 imports the canonical morpheus-runtime-catalog.json) and can drift.
findings.fix: Have the emergency shim derive its advertised capability fields from the same packages/shared public-runtime catalog the gateway uses (import morpheus-runtime-catalog.json) instead of hard-coding, and expose a single `x-morpheus-runtime` discriminator that both surfaces honor so a status consumer can tell which is authoritative. Lowest-cost win: document/emit in the gateway runtime-status whether it is serving from the box origin or the Vercel emergency shim so the two never silently disagree.
findings.risk: Mostly observability/DX; no money-path impact. The shim is a fallback by design.

findings.subsystem: apps/web (web-dx): public HTTP API surface, docs, status/health pages, onboarding, error responses, accessibility, web perf
findings.title: Quickstart "seal your parameters" step calls a function that does not exist (encryptWithOracleX25519) while a real, working helper ships in the codebase
findings.evidence: apps/web/app/docs/quickstart/page.tsx:113 and apps/web/app/docs/oracle/page.tsx:205 both show `const ciphertext = await encryptWithOracleX25519(JSON.stringify(secrets), public_key)`. grep across app/components/lib finds ZERO definition or import of `encryptWithOracleX25519` — it is a placeholder. Meanwhile apps/web/lib/browser-encryption.ts:70/112 implements exactly the documented scheme (`encryptTextWithOraclePublicKey` / `encryptJsonWithOraclePublicKey`, X25519+HKDF-SHA256+AES-256-GCM, ENVELOPE_ALGORITHM='X25519-HKDF-SHA256-AES-256-GCM'). The quickstart promises integration "in under 5 minutes" but the single hardest step (correct payload sealing) references a non-existent symbol, so a copy-paste developer fails immediately.
findings.fix: In quickstart/page.tsx and oracle/page.tsx, replace the fictional `encryptWithOracleX25519` snippet with the real helper (import from a published `@neo-morpheus-oracle/shared` browser-encryption export, or inline the body of `encryptTextWithOraclePublicKey`). Better: export `encryptTextWithOraclePublicKey`/`encryptJsonWithOraclePublicKey` from packages/shared so external consumers can `npm i` it, and document that import in the snippet.
findings.risk: Docs/snippet change plus optionally promoting an existing function to a shared package export. No runtime behavior change to the app.

findings.subsystem: apps/web (web-dx): public HTTP API surface, docs, status/health pages, onboarding, error responses, accessibility, web perf
findings.title: Sealed-envelope wire format is never documented, so the encryption step is unreproducible without reading source
findings.evidence: The worker expects a base64-wrapped JSON envelope `{v:2, alg, epk, iv, ct, tag}` with HKDF info string 'morpheus-confidential-payload-v2' and salt = recipient public key (apps/web/lib/browser-encryption.ts:1-3,51-67,98-109). Docs mention only the algorithm name 'HKDF' in prose (1 mention each in quickstart/page.tsx and lib/docs-data.ts) and never describe the envelope fields, the HKDF info/salt binding, the 12-byte IV, or the AES-GCM tag split. A developer in a non-JS language (or not using the unpublished helper) cannot construct a valid ciphertext.
findings.fix: Add a section to docs/api-reference (or docs/oracle) documenting the exact sealed-envelope schema: field names (v/alg/epk/iv/ct/tag), encodings (raw X25519 pubkey base64, 12-byte IV, 16-byte GCM tag, ciphertext-without-tag), HKDF parameters (salt=recipient pubkey, info='morpheus-confidential-payload-v2'+epk+rpk), and the outer base64(JSON) wrapping. Source it from the ENVELOPE_* constants so it stays in sync.
findings.risk: Pure documentation addition.

findings.subsystem: apps/web (web-dx): public HTTP API surface, docs, status/health pages, onboarding, error responses, accessibility, web perf
findings.title: Public HTTP API (rate-limited REST endpoints) is essentially undocumented in the API Reference
findings.evidence: `/api/oracle/query`, `/api/oracle/smart-fetch`, `/api/compute/execute`, `/api/feeds/status`, `/api/confidential/store` are wrapped in `createRateLimitedHandler` with per-IP limits and X-RateLimit-* headers (apps/web/app/api/oracle/query/route.ts:78, smart-fetch/route.ts:78, compute/execute/route.ts:57, feeds/status/route.ts), making them a deliberate public API. Yet docs/api-reference/page.tsx lists ZERO REST endpoints (grep for POST | /api/oracle | /api/feeds | /api/compute returns nothing) — it only covers the Neo N3 contract call pattern + the NeoDID resolve GET. There is no OpenAPI/Swagger file anywhere in the repo. Request/response bodies, the `degraded`/`unavailable` fallback shapes (feeds/[symbol]/route.ts:19-37, oracle/public-key/route.ts:45-60), rate limits, and error codes are discoverable only by reading source.
findings.fix: Add an HTTP API section to docs/api-reference documenting each public endpoint: method, path, request schema, success response, the degraded/fallback response shape, rate limit (req/min) and X-RateLimit headers. Optionally ship a static openapi.json served from /public and a Swagger/Scalar viewer route. At minimum a curl example per endpoint.
findings.risk: Documentation work; needs accuracy review against each route handler. No code-path risk.

findings.subsystem: apps/web (web-dx): public HTTP API surface, docs, status/health pages, onboarding, error responses, accessibility, web perf
findings.title: Public POST endpoints return free-form `{error: string}` while a machine-readable `{error, error_code}` helper exists and is used only by one route — inconsistent error contract
findings.evidence: apps/web/lib/api-helpers.ts exports `apiError(message, code, status)` returning `{error, error_code}` (line 9-11) but it is used ONLY in app/api/confidential/store/route.ts (codes UNAUTHORIZED, MISSING_CIPHERTEXT, etc.). The main public endpoints use `badRequest()` which returns `{error: message}` with no code (oracle/query/route.ts:29,75; oracle/smart-fetch/route.ts:29,75; feeds/[symbol]/route.ts:56,100; compute/execute/route.ts:31 returns yet another inline `{error}`). Worse, on the proxy path the body is whatever the upstream nitro worker emitted (lib/nitro.ts:174 passes through `lastResponse.text`), so a caller sees three different error shapes from the same API family with no stable, programmatic discriminator.
findings.fix: Standardize: have `badRequest` (or a new `apiError`) always emit `{error, error_code}` and give the validation failures in oracle/query, smart-fetch, feeds/[symbol], compute/execute stable codes (INVALID_JSON_BODY, UNSUPPORTED_CHAIN, PROVIDER_CONFIG_ERROR, etc.). Document the code set. Keep upstream pass-through but wrap non-2xx upstream bodies in a consistent envelope or at least add an `error_code: 'UPSTREAM_ERROR'` + upstream_status (the public-key/feeds fallbacks already do this — generalize it).
findings.risk: Changing response bodies can break existing consumers/tests that assert on `{error}` only; the api-helpers comment at line 21-26 deliberately kept `badRequest` byte-identical. Do additively (add error_code, keep error) and update tests.

findings.title: Stale TEE provider references ("Phala") in developer-facing docs after the AWS Nitro migration

This contradicts the rest of the product (status page says 'Oracle CVM Runtime', config is all NITRO_*) and misleads a developer trying to understand/verify the trust model.
findings.fix: Sweep these strings: change 'Phala TEE'/'Phala deployment'/'Phala CVMs' to the current Nitro/CVM terminology (or a provider-neutral 'TEE/CVM'). For the verifier page, reconcile the attestation fields actually verified by lib/attestation.ts and the /api/attestation/verify route (which still accepts expected_compose_hash/expected_app_id) with whatever Nitro emits, so the verifier guide matches reality. Update OverviewTab.tsx:93 log label.

findings.subsystem: apps/web (web-dx): public HTTP API surface, docs, status/health pages, onboarding, error responses, accessibility, web perf
findings.title: Code-snippet copy button has no error handling or insecure-context fallback; copy silently no-ops in some environments
findings.evidence: apps/web/components/ui/CodeBlock.tsx:30-34 `handleCopy` calls `navigator.clipboard.writeText(code)` unawaited with no try/catch. On HTTP (non-secure context), in iframes without clipboard permission, or when the user denies permission, `navigator.clipboard` is undefined or rejects -> unhandled promise rejection and the 'Copied' confirmation still flips on (setCopied(true) runs regardless), so the developer believes the copy succeeded when it did not. The primary way devs grab the quickstart snippets.
findings.fix: Make handleCopy async, await `navigator.clipboard.writeText` inside try/catch, only setCopied(true) on success, and on failure fall back to a hidden textarea + document.execCommand('copy') or show a toast 'Copy failed — select manually'. Guard for `navigator.clipboard` undefined.
findings.risk: Minimal; pure client UX hardening.

findings.subsystem: apps/web (web-dx): public HTTP API surface, docs, status/health pages, onboarding, error responses, accessibility, web perf

findings.fix: Add a visually-hidden-until-focused skip link as the first child of <body> (or top of Layout) pointing to `#main-content`, and add `id="main-content"` to the <main> at Layout.tsx:218. Trivial CSS for the .skip-link focus reveal.

findings.subsystem: apps/web (web-dx): public HTTP API surface, docs, status/health pages, onboarding, error responses, accessibility, web perf
findings.title: Quickstart pins a single hardcoded relay tx hash and docs-data pins a past automation start_at, guaranteeing rot
findings.evidence: apps/web/app/docs/quickstart/page.tsx:75-77 hardcodes 'Latest full-path relay tx: 0x057d4a58...' presented as 'Live ... Validation', which becomes stale the moment a newer validation runs and there is no mechanism to refresh it. lib/docs-data.ts:187 AUTOMATION_PATTERNS sets `start_at: '2026-03-11T00:10:00Z'`, a date already in the past, shown as an example config a developer might copy verbatim into an interval trigger.
findings.fix: For the relay tx, either remove the specific hash (link to the explorer filtered by the consumer instead) or pull a recent example from the live /explorer data. For docs-data.ts:187, use a relative/template value (e.g. '<ISO timestamp in the future>' or compute now()+offset) so the copied example is not pre-expired.
